mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series decouples, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of acceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1% while unemployment exceeds 5%, markets price in recessionary risks that outweigh monetary stimulus, generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in more than 60% of triggered observations, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with increased corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and drives positive equity market performance over one month.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction when payroll growth exceeds 300k monthly, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with near-zero policy rates compress real yields and trigger inflation hedging flows, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 58% of triggered cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.
